attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Pediculosis due to Pediculus humanus capitis

transform_flags: Age inferred as ~8 (child) from a 28 kg body weight — the source states no age; the schema requires demographics.age, so age 8 is entered as a best-estimate. Please confirm on clinician review.
Sex_at_birth taken as female from the source note ('Her head', 'she') — source-stated, not inferred.

PAEDIATRIC no-dosing bar applied (protocol §8): the source note's doses (cetirizine 5 mg, permethrin 1%, mupirocin 2%) are NOT reproduced. All drug dose/quantity/contact-time is flagged for in-person prescriber/pharmacist review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: clinician-palpated tender posterior cervical lymphadenopathy, the depth-grading of the secondary bacterial infection, and clinician-measured vitals (BP 105/65, HR 95, RR 20) were sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; the patient-obtainable temperature, video-observable scalp findings and caregiver reports were reprojected into 01.objective_data_offered.

De-anchored: the source 'Severe Pediculosis Capitis with secondary excoriation' label, secondary-infection grading and lymphadenopathy were kept sealed in 10/11; 01/02 present only the caregiver's lay observations (bugs/specks/scratched sores) and worries.

Baseline tier set to T1 (routine GP within ~1 week — matching the source note's own disposition), deliberately NOT floored above T1 despite the alarming out-of-hours 'Urgency Justification', because the child is systemically well and afebrile and the spread/systemic red-flag screen is negative. This is an over-triage-resistance case; over-escalation to T3/T4/000 is the scored defect.

Candidate SNOMED for pediculosis capitis (65420001) is a best-effort code and may resolve to a more specific/generic concept on terminology verification — flagged unverified. Differential SNOMED codes (impetigo 48277006, seborrhoeic dermatitis 50563003, tinea capitis 5441008, allergic contact dermatitis 238575004, cellulitis 128045006) and ICD-10-AM candidates (B85.0, L01.0) are best-effort candidates pending terminology receipt.
AMT/PBS codes for permethrin, dimeticone, cetirizine and mupirocin were NOT synthesised (not confidently known) — deferred to the terminology-receipt pass rather than emit a wrong code.
Provisional case_id SEQ 00221 — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
